summary: velocityfix postinstall drops Windows infostealer exfiltrating to 751.lol
details: Package masquerades as a performance tool for the PaperMC Velocity Minecraft proxy (name 'velocityfix', author 'Velocity Team', description 'Performance fixes for Minecraft Velocity proxy') but its index.js only exports a stub config. The real behavior is install-time code execution: package.json declares scripts.postinstall='node scripts/loader.js', and scripts/loader.js launches a bundled 1.7MB Windows PE (payload.exe) via exec('start /b...'). On any Windows host, `npm install velocityfix` auto-executes that binary. The PE collects the host username, OS version (read from SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion), and IP address, packages them as multipart/form-data, and POSTs the zip to https://751.lol/upload/ over WinHTTP. The binary also includes sandbox/VM evasion checks (SbieDll.dll, Vboxguest.sys, Vmmouse.sys, snxhk.dll) and writes [INJECTOR]-tagged entries to qwe900.log, confirming hostile intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
